name: PR Review
description: Transcribed as data (#1284 Packet 1) from build_review_graph (crates/darkmux-lab/src/lab/review.rs), the Task/Step graph `darkmux mission launch review` builds (retired the `pr-review run` verb in Packet 4b): investigate (resolve bundles from the diff, probe every staffed seat as seats x k generic dispatch.map steps (#1442), dedup the flags with domain reconstruction at the dedup boundary) -> adjudicate (double-confirm judge over the deduped flags) -> report (verify confirmed findings, synthesize tier counts). As of Packet 3, this document is the EXECUTABLE source of truth -- build_review_graph is a thin launcher that loads this config, resolves staffing into the probe-template task's `expand` collection, and calls mission_config::interpret. `src/mission_launch_review.rs` is the operator-facing CALLER of that launcher (`--param key=value` inputs below), not a second graph builder.

inputs.name: diff_file
inputs.description: Path to the unified diff file under review. `mission_launch_review::launch` reads it into ReviewStepContext.diff and feeds it to every probe/judge/verify dispatch. Required unconditionally -- even a `from_envelope` synthesis-only launch needs it, to resolve each finding's quoted anchor back to a diff line.

inputs.name: case_id
inputs.description: Unique id for this review run (ReviewStepContext.case_id) -- correlates flow records and the review envelope, and seeds the minted mission's instance id. LAUNCHER-DERIVED when absent: `owner/repo@head_sha` for a GitHub source, the worktree path, or `local` -- an explicit value always wins. Never asked of the operator by the generic missing-inputs gate (mirrors `coder-phase`'s `mission_id` input).

inputs.name: worktree
inputs.description: Local checkout directory to bundle from. Alternative to `github` + `head_sha` (mutually exclusive; exactly one source is required unless `from_envelope`).

inputs.name: github
inputs.description: GitHub source `owner/repo` -- bundles via the GitHub API, no local checkout. Requires `head_sha`.

inputs.name: head_sha
inputs.description: Commit SHA to read file content at (GitHub source only).

inputs.name: intent_file
inputs.description: Path to a file carrying the author's stated intent for the change (PR title + description, one blob), fed into the JUDGE prompt only (ReviewStepContext.intent_body; intent_title stays empty -- matches the retired CLI). Absent -> "(no description provided)".

inputs.name: review-probe-high
inputs.description: (#1475 packet 3) Per-run role->profile OVERRIDE for the high-recall probe seat (`--param review-probe-high=<profile>`). The named profile must exist in the registry; its default model staffs the seat for this run only. Precedence: this override > the machine-local `role_profiles` map in config.json > `default_profile`. Absent -> the map (else the fresh-user default_profile floor).

inputs.name: review-probe-mid
inputs.description: (#1475 packet 3) Per-run role->profile OVERRIDE for the mid-recall probe seat (`--param review-probe-mid=<profile>`). Same precedence + semantics as `review-probe-high`.

inputs.name: review-probe-low
inputs.description: (#1475 packet 3) Per-run role->profile OVERRIDE for the low-recall probe seat (`--param review-probe-low=<profile>`). Same precedence + semantics as `review-probe-high`.

inputs.name: review-judge
inputs.description: (#1475 packet 3) Per-run role->profile OVERRIDE for the judge seat (`--param review-judge=<profile>`). Same precedence + semantics as `review-probe-high` -- override the machine's `role_profiles` binding for one run without editing config.json (e.g. escalate the judge to a denser profile).

inputs.name: review-verify
inputs.description: (#1475 packet 3) Per-run role->profile OVERRIDE for the verify seat (`--param review-verify=<profile>`). Same precedence + semantics as `review-probe-high`.

inputs.name: request_changes
inputs.description: (#1426 ship-2) When truthy (`true`/`1`/`yes`/`on`), confirmed findings render as a blocking REQUEST_CHANGES review; else (default) an advisory COMMENT. Replaces the retired crew's `request_changes` field.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.description: Execution mode: sequential | parallel | auto (default auto). `auto` lets the gestalt wave planner decide co-residency from the resolved seats; the value is resolved via `resolve_mode` and recorded on the envelope as `mode` provenance, not a behavioral switch the operator normally sets.

inputs.name: passes
inputs.description: (#1266) The judge seat's consensus DEPTH (passes) — 1 single / 2 double-confirm / N unanimous. Governs the JUDGE seat only (the sole consumer; the probe/verify seats ignore it). Absent -> the resolver's default (2, double-confirm). Validated `>= 1` at resolution time (a zero-pass judge makes no ruling); lenient on read otherwise (contract 7). Threaded to the already-parameterized `multi_pass_confirm` pass count via `ResolvedSeatStaffing.passes`.

inputs.name: envelope_out
inputs.description: Path to write the full review envelope (pretty JSON).

inputs.name: emit
inputs.description: Path to write the rendered `{mode, review, comment}` payload. `-` (or omitted) writes to stdout -- a CONTRACT the CI workflow parses byte-for-byte, so `mission launch` prints no decorative banner ahead of it for this config.

inputs.name: profiles
inputs.description: Override path for profiles.json (else DARKMUX_PROFILES / the default resolution).

inputs.name: attribution
inputs.description: Attribution text for the posted footer (#1113) -- the default derives "where the model ran" from the envelope's own member records.

inputs.name: bundler
inputs.description: External bundler command (`<cmd> --worktree <dir> --diff <file>`), standing in for the built-in bundler.

inputs.name: charges_file
inputs.description: Re-judge a previously-recorded flag list without re-running the probe (run_judge_only) -- the bundler still runs, since the judge needs the code each flag's bundle_id refers to. Mints NO mission (same as the retired CLI).

inputs.name: from_envelope
inputs.description: Synthesis-only: read a previously-written envelope_out and emit the rendered payload -- zero model calls, zero bundling, zero mission minted. The CI-testable path.

inputs.name: intent_title
inputs.description: INTERNALLY DERIVED, never operator-supplied -- `mission_launch_review::run_dispatch` always passes an empty string (matches the retired CLI's own RunOpts shape, which carried one intent blob with no separate title field). ReviewStepContext.intent_title.

inputs.name: staffing
inputs.description: Resolved probe/judge/verify seat staffing (StaffingSnapshot / ResolvedSeatStaffing), computed by `mission_launch_review::run_dispatch` from the role->profile resolver (#1475 -- each review role resolves via launch override > role_profiles map > default_profile), never a literal CLI input. The probe-template task expands over the three distinct probe roles (review-probe-high/-mid/-low), one task per role. See the 'review-probe-template' task's notes below.

inputs.name: judge_concurrency
inputs.description: The judge step's real config.concurrency, resolved by the launcher from darkmux_types::config_access::review_judge_concurrency() (env > config.review.judge_concurrency > 1) -- never a literal CLI input. ALWAYS supplied by the launcher as a step_config_override on review-judge-step -- the static `config.concurrency: 1` below is a documented DEFAULT for a human reading this file, never load-bearing at launch.

phases.description: resolve review bundles from the diff, probe every staffed seat, dedup

tasks.description: resolve review bundles from the diff

TEMPLATE for the probe stage. build_review_graph builds ONE task/step PER PROBE ROLE (#1475 packet 2 — the role->profile flip): the expansion is over the three distinct probe roles (review-probe-high / -mid / -low), mint-time-known, not a resolver-fed seat list. Each expanded copy binds its own role via `role_pattern` (the item IS the probe role id), so the crew is the task->role->profile->model rollup -- recall diversity falls out of three distinct role bindings, not k draws of one scored model. build_review_graph stamps each expanded step's dispatch config (model resolved from that role's role_profiles binding, ids `review-probe-{index}-task` / `review-probe-{index}-step`).
dedup probe flags — mechanism-family keying + anchor matching
double-confirm judge — internal pass1/pass2 loop over deduped flags

phases.description: verify confirmed findings, synthesize tier counts

tasks.description: verify — render frozen per-finding prompts, then adjudicate confirmed findings via the generic dispatch.map
notes: always present in the graph even when no verify seat is staffed -- the render step (review.verify-render) emits an EMPTY collection in that case (and on a judge-doomed run, and on a zero-confirmed docket), which makes the dispatch.map step a completed no-op with residency None: zero model loads, zero dispatches (#1442, the generic port of the empty-docket short-circuit).

the render step consumes the judge task's output and emits one frozen verify_prompt per CONFIRMED finding (byte parity with the #1256 golden by construction); build_review_graph stamps the map step's dispatch config (model/system/max_tokens/bucket_budget/...) only when a verify seat is staffed. The verify-APPLY boundary (state machine, member accounting, budget row) lives in the synthesis step.

tasks.description: synthesis — apply verify adjudications, finalize tier counts + needs-check clustering
